Package ru.nsu.krasnyanskii.pizzeria

Class PizzaStorage

java.lang.Object
ru.nsu.krasnyanskii.pizzeria.PizzaStorage
public class PizzaStorage extends Object
Thread-safe bounded storage for cooked pizzas.

Uses intrinsic lock (synchronized + wait/notifyAll) without java.util.concurrent collections.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    PizzaStorage(int capacity)
    Creates a PizzaStorage.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    closeAccepting()
    Signals that no more pizzas will be added; couriers will drain and exit.
    List<Order>
    drainAll()
    Drains all remaining pizzas (used during shutdown serialization).
    boolean
    isAccepting()
    Returns true if the storage is still accepting new pizzas.
    boolean
    isEmpty()
    Returns true if no pizzas are in storage.
    void
    put(Order order)
    Puts a pizza into storage, blocking if full.
    int
    size()
    Returns the number of pizzas currently in storage.
    List<Order>
    take(int maxCount)
    Takes up to maxCount pizzas, blocking while storage is empty and open.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • PizzaStorage

      public PizzaStorage(int capacity)
      Creates a PizzaStorage.
      Parameters:
      capacity - max number of pizzas; must be positive
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if capacity is not positive

  • Method Details

    • put

      public void put(Order order) throws InterruptedException
      Puts a pizza into storage, blocking if full.
      Parameters:
      order - cooked order to store
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the thread is interrupted while waiting

    • take

      public List<Order> take(int maxCount) throws InterruptedException
      Takes up to maxCount pizzas, blocking while storage is empty and open.
      Parameters:
      maxCount - max pizzas to take
      Returns:
      list of taken pizzas; empty if storage is closed and empty
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the thread is interrupted while waiting

    • closeAccepting

      public void closeAccepting()
      Signals that no more pizzas will be added; couriers will drain and exit.

    • drainAll

      public List<Order> drainAll()
      Drains all remaining pizzas (used during shutdown serialization).
      Returns:
      snapshot of remaining orders

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if no pizzas are in storage.
      Returns:
      true if empty

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of pizzas currently in storage.
      Returns:
      current size

    • isAccepting

      public boolean isAccepting()
      Returns true if the storage is still accepting new pizzas.
      Returns:
      true if accepting
